top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
55ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Via del Sale's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
Gran Fondo Via del Sale's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
At Gran Fondo Via del Sale, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Via del Sale. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Via del Sale, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 87 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.heat: March in Cervia, Emilia-Romagna, Italy can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Gran Fondo Via del Sale. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Via del Sale’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Via del Sale. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Via del Sale requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 87 miles of mixed terrain in Cervia, Emilia-Romagna, Italy.
